author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter I. 33.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have arrived, as the Lord hath willed it, to the day of our promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will grant this also, that we may arrive at the fulfillment of the promise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then those things which we say, if they are useful to us and to you, are from Him; but those things which proceed from man are false, as our Lord Jesus Christ Himself has said, “He that speaketh a lie speaketh of his own.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one has anything of his own except falsehood and sin.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if man has any truth and justice, it is from that fountain after which we ought to thirst in this desert, so that being, as it were, bedewed by some drops from it, and comforted in the meantime in this pilgrimage, we may not fail by the way, but reach His rest and satisfying fullness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then “he that speaketh a lie speaketh of his own,” he who speaketh the truth speaketh of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John is true, Christ is the Truth; John is true, but every true man is true from the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, John is true, and a man cannot be true except from the Truth, from whom was he true, unless from Him who said, “I am the truth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Truth, then, could not speak contrary to the true man, or the true man contrary to the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Truth sent the true man, and he was true because sent by the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it was the Truth that sent John, then it was Christ that sent him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that which Christ does with the Father, the Father does; and what the Father does with Christ, Christ does.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Father does nothing apart from the Son, nor the Son anything apart from the Father: inseparable love, inseparable unity: inseparable majesty, inseparable power, according to these words which He Himself propounded, “I and my Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then sent John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we say the Father, we speak truly; if we say the Son, we speak truly; but to speak more plainly, we say the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But whom the Father and the Son sent, one God sent; because the Son said, “I and the Father are one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, did he not know Him by whom he was sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he said, “I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I interrogate John: “Who sent thee to baptize with water? what did He say to thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it this, O John, that He said to thee who sent thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is manifest that it was this; who, then, sent thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps the Father.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: True God is the Father, and the Truth is God the Son: if the Father without the Son sent thee, God without the Truth sent thee; but if thou art true, because thou dost speak the truth, and dost speak of the Truth, the Father did not send thee without the Son, but the Father and the Son together sent thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, the Son sent thee with the Father, how didst thou not know Him by whom thou wast sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He whom thou hadst seen in the Truth, Himself sent thee that He might be recognized in the flesh, and said, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did John hear this that he might know Him whom he had not known, or that he might more fully know Him whom he had already known?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he had been entirely ignorant of Him, he would not have said to Him when He came to the river to be baptized, “I have need to be baptized of Thee, and comest Thou to me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew Him therefore.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when did the dove descend?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord had been baptized, and was ascending from the water.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if He who sent Him said, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost,” and he knew Him not, but when the dove descended he learned to know Him, and the time at which the dove descended was when the Lord was going up from the water; but John had known the Lord, when the Lord came to him to the water: it is made plain to us that John after a manner knew, and after a manner did not at first know the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And unless we understand it so, he was a liar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How was he true acknowledging the Lord and saying, “Comest Thou to me to be baptized,” and, “I have need to be baptized of Thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is he true when he said this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how is he again true when he saith, “I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He who baptizeth with the Holy Ghost”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord was made known by a dove, not to him who knew Him not, but to him who in a manner knew Him, and in a manner knew Him not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is for us to discover what, in Him, John did not know, and learned by the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why was John sent baptizing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already, I recollect, I have explained that to you, beloved, according to my ability.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the baptism of John was necessary for our salvation, it ought even now to be used.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we cannot think that men are not saved now, or that more are not saved now, or that there was one salvation then, another now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Christ has been changed, the salvation has also been changed; if salvation is in Christ, and Christ Himself is the same, there is the same salvation to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why was John sent baptizing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it behoved Christ to be baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did it behove Christ to be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did it behove Christ to be born?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did it behove Christ to be crucified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He had come to point out the way of humility, and to make Himself the way of humility; in all things had humility to be fulfilled by Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He deigned from this to give authority to His own baptism, that His servants might know with what alacrity they ought to run to the baptism of the Lord, when He Himself did not refuse to receive the baptism of a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This favor was bestowed upon John that it should be called his baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed to this, exercise your discrimination, and know it, beloved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The baptism which John received is called the baptism of John: alone he received such a gift.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No one of the just before him and no one after him so received a baptism that it should be called his baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He received it indeed, for of himself he could do nothing: for if any one speaketh of his own, he speaketh of his own a lie.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence did he receive it except from the Lord Jesus Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From Him he received power to baptize whom he afterwards baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not marvel; for Christ acted in the same manner in respect to John as in respect to His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For concerning Christ it was said, “All things were made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If all things were made by him, Mary also was made by Him, of whom Christ was afterwards born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, beloved; in the same manner that He did create Mary, and was created by Mary, so did He give the baptism of John, and was baptized by John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this purpose therefore did He receive baptism from John, in order that, receiving what was inferior from an inferior, He might exhort inferiors to receive that which was superior.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore was not He alone baptized by John, if John, by whom Christ was baptized, was sent for this end, to prepare a way for the Lord, that is, for Christ Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This we have already explained, but we recur to it, because it is necessary for the present question.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If our Lord Jesus Christ had been alone baptized with the baptism of John;—hold fast what we say; let not the world have such power as to efface from your hearts what the Spirit of God has written there; let not the thorns of care have such power as to choke the seed which is being sown in you: for why are we compelled to repeat the same things, but because we are not sure of the memory of your hearts?—and if then the Lord alone had been baptized with the baptism of John, there would be persons who would so reckon it, that the baptism of John was greater than is the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they would say, that baptism is so much the greater, that Christ alone deserved to be baptized with it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, that an example of humility might be given us by the Lord, that the salvation of baptism might be obtained by us, Christ accepted what for Him was not necessary, but on our account was necessary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And again, lest that which Christ received from John should be preferred to the baptism of Christ, others also were permitted to be baptized by John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But for those who were baptized by John that baptism did not suffice: for they were baptized with the baptism of Christ; because the baptism of John was not the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who receive the baptism of Christ do not seek the baptism of John; those who received the baptism of John sought the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore was the baptism of John sufficient for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How should it not be sufficient, when not even it was necessary?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For to Him was no baptism necessary; but in order to exhort us to receive His baptism, He received the baptism of His servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And lest the baptism of the servant should be preferred to the baptism of the Lord, other fellow-servants were baptized with the baptism of the servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it behoved those fellow-servants who were baptized with that baptism to be likewise baptized with the baptism of the Lord: but those who were baptized with the baptism of the Lord do not require the baptism of the fellow-servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, then, John had accepted a baptism which may be properly called the baptism of John, but the Lord Jesus Christ would not give His baptism to any, not that no one should be baptized with the baptism of the Lord, but that the Lord Himself should always baptize: that was done, that the Lord should baptize by means of servants; that is to say, those whom the servants of the Lord were to baptize, the Lord baptized, not they.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is one thing to baptize in the capacity of a servant, another thing to baptize with power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For baptism derives its character from Him through whose power it is given; not from him through whose ministry it is given.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As was John, so was his baptism: the righteous baptism of a righteous man; but of a man who had received from the Lord that grace, and so great grace, that he was worthy to be the forerunner of the Judge, and to point Him out with the finger, and to fulfill the saying of that prophecy: “The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way for the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As was the Lord, such was His baptism: the baptism of the Lord, then, was divine, because the Lord was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord Jesus Christ could, if He wished, have given power to one of His servants to give a baptism of his own, as it were, in His stead, and have transferred from Himself the power of baptizing, and assigned it to one of His servants, and have given the same power to the baptism transferred to the servant as it had when bestowed by the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He would not do, in order that the hope of the baptized might be in him by whom they acknowledged themselves to have been baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would not, therefore, that the servant should place his hope in the servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And therefore the apostle exclaimed, when he saw men wishing to place their hope in himself, “Was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Paul then baptized as a servant, not as the power itself; but the Lord baptized as the power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was both able to give this power to His servants, and unwilling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He had given this power to His servants—that is to say, that what belonged to the Lord should be theirs—there would have been as many baptisms as servants; so that, as we speak of the baptism of John, we should also have spoken of the baptism of Peter, the baptism of Paul, the baptism of James, the baptism of Thomas, of Matthew, of Bartholomew: for we spoke of that baptism as that of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps some one objects, and says, Prove to us that that baptism was called the baptism of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I will prove it from the very words of the Truth Himself, when He asked the Jews, “The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, lest as many baptisms should be spoken of as there are servants who received power from the Lord to baptize, the Lord kept to Himself the power of baptizing, and gave to His servants the ministry.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The servant says that he baptizes; he says so rightly, as the apostle says, “And I baptized also the household of Stephanas;” but as a servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, if even he be bad, and he happen to have the ministration of baptism, and if men do not know him, but God knows him, God, who has kept the power to Himself, permits baptism to be administered through him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this John did not know in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He was the Lord he knew, and that he ought to be baptized by Him he knew; and he confessed that He was the Truth, and that he, the true man, was sent by the Truth: this he knew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what was in Him which he knew not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That he was about to retain to Himself the power of His baptism, and was not to transmit or transfer it to any servant; but that, whether a good servant baptized in a ministerial manner, or whether an evil servant baptized, the person baptized should not know that he was baptized, unless by Him who kept to Himself the power of baptizing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that you may know, brethren, what John did not know in Him, he learned it by means of the dove: for he knew the Lord; but that He was to retain to Himself the power of baptizing, and not to give it to any servant, he did not yet know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Regarding this he said, “I knew Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that you may know that he there learnt this, give heed to what follows: “But He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What same is He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he already knew the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose, then, that John had said thus far, “I knew Him not: but He that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me—” We ask, what He said?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It follows: “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not say what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the meantime give heed: “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what same is He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did He who sent me mean to teach me by means of a dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That He was Himself the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already I knew by whom I was sent; already I knew Him to whom I said, “Comest Thou to me to be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So far, then, did I know the Lord, that I wished to be baptized by Him, not that He should be baptized by me; and then He said to me, “Suffer it to be so now; for thus it becometh us to fulfill all righteousness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I came to suffer; do I not come to be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Let all righteousness be fulfilled,” says my God to me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let all righteousness be fulfilled; let me teach entire humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I know that there will be proud ones in my future people; I know that some men then will be eminent in some grace, so that when they see ordinary persons baptized, they, because they consider themselves better, whether in continence, or in alms-giving, or in doctrine, will perhaps not deign to receive what has been received by their inferiors.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was needful that I should heal them, so that they should not disdain to come to the baptism of the Lord, because I came to the baptism of the servant.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already, then, John knew this, and he knew the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did the dove teach?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did He desire to teach by means of the dove—that is, by means of the Holy Spirit thus coming to teach who had sent him to whom He said, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is this He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But didst thou already know this, that the same Lord having the power to baptize, was not to give that power to any servant, but to retain it to Himself, so that all who were baptized by the ministration of the servant, should not impute their baptism to the servant, but to the Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Didst thou already know this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I did not know this: so what did He say to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding upon Him, the same is He who baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not say, “He is the Lord;” He does not say, “He is the Christ;” He does not say, “He is God;” He does not say, “He is Jesus;” He does not say, “He is the One who was born of the Virgin Mary, after thee, before thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This He does not say, for this John did already know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what did he not know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That this great authority of baptism the Lord Himself was to have, and to retain to Himself, whether present in the earth or absent in body in the heaven, and present in majesty; lest Paul should say, my baptism; lest Peter should say, my baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore see, give heed to the words of the apostles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: None of the apostles said, my baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although there was one gospel of all, yet thou findest that they said, my gospel: thou dost not find that they say, my baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, my brethren, John learned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What John learned by means of the dove let us also learn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the dove did not teach John without teaching the Church, the Church to which it was said, “My dove is one.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the dove teach the dove; let the dove know what John learned by the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Holy Spirit descended in the form of a dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this which John learned in the dove, wherefore did he learn it in the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it behoved him to learn, and perhaps it did not so much behove him to learn as to learn by the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What shall I say, my brethren, concerning the dove? or when will faculty of tongue or heart suffice to speak as I wish?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And perchance, my wish falls short of my duty in speaking; even if I were able to speak as I wish, how much less am I able to speak as I ought?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I could wish to hear one better than myself speak this, rather than speak of it to you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John learns to know Him whom he knew; but he learns in Him with regard to what he did not know; with regard to what he did know, he does not learn.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what did he know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did he not know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That the power of the Lord’s baptism was not to pass from the Lord to any man, but that the ministration of it plainly would do so; the power from the Lord to no one, the ministration both to good and bad.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not the dove shrink from the ministration of the bad, but have regard to the power of the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What injury does a bad servant do to you where the Lord is good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What impediment can the malicious herald put in your way if the judge is well-disposed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John learned by means of the dove this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it that he learned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him repeat it himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The same said unto me,” saith he, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending as a dove, and abiding on Him, this is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not those seducers deceive thee, O dove, who say, We baptize.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Acknowledge, dove, what the dove has taught: “This is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By means of the dove we are taught that this is He; and dost thou think that thou art baptized by his authority by whose ministration thou art baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou thinkest this, thou art not as yet in the body of the dove; and if thou art not in the body of the dove, it is not to be wondered at that thou hast not simplicity; for by means of the dove, simplicity is chiefly designated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, my brethren, by the simplicity of the dove did John learn that “This is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost,” unless to show that these are not doves who have scattered the Church?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hawks they were, and kites.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove does not tear.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thou seest that they hold us up to hatred, for the persecutions, as they call them, which they have suffered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bodily persecutions, indeed, if they are to be so called, they have suffered, since these were the scourges of the Lord, plainly administering temporal correction, lest He should have to condemn them eternally, if they did not acknowledge it and amend themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They truly persecute the Church who persecute by means of deceit; they strike the heart more heavily who strike with the sword of the tongue; they shed blood more bitterly who, as far as they can, slay Christ in man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They seem to be in fear, as it were, of the judgment of the authorities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does the authority do to thee if thou art good? but if thou art evil, fear the authority; “For he beareth not the sword in vain,” saith the apostle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Draw not the sword wherewith thou dost strike Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christian, what dost thou persecute in a Christian?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did the Emperor persecute in thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He persecuted the flesh; thou in a Christian persecutest the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou dost not slay the flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, nevertheless, they do not spare the flesh; as many as they were able, they slew with the sword; they spared neither their own nor strangers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is known to all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The authority is hated because it is legitimate; he acts in a hated manner who acts according to the law; he acts without incurring hatred who acts contrary to the laws.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give heed, each one of you, my brethren, to what the Christian possesses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His humanity he has in common with many, his Christianity distinguishes him from many, and his Christianity belongs to him more strictly than his humanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, as a Christian, he is renewed after the image of God, by whom man was made after the image of God; but as a man he might be bad, he might be a pagan, he might be an idolater.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This thou dost persecute in the Christian, which is his better part; for this by which he lives thou wishest to take away from him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he lives tempo rally according to the spirit of life, by which his body is animated, but he lives for eternity according to the baptism which he received from the Lord; thou wishest to take this away from him which he received from the Lord, this thou wishest to take away from him by which he lives.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Robbers, with regard to those whom they wish to despoil, have the purpose to enrich themselves and to deprive their victims of all that they have; but thou takest from him, and with thee there will not be anything more, for there does not accrue more to thee because thou takest from him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, truly, they do the same as those who take away the natural life: they take it away from another, and yet they themselves have not two lives.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, dost thou wish to take away?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What displeases thee in the man whom thou wishest to rebaptize?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art not able to give what he already has, but thou makest him deny what he has.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What greater cruelty did the pagan persecutor of the Church commit?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Swords were stretched out against the martyrs, wild beasts were let loose, fires were applied: for what purpose these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In order that the sufferer might be induced to say, I am not a Christian.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What dost thou teach him whom thou wishest to rebaptize, unless that he first say, I am not a Christian?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the same purpose for which the persecutor put forth the flame, thou puttest forth the tongue; thou dost by seducing what he did not do by slaying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is it thou dost give, and to whom art thou to give it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he tells thee the truth, and does not lie, seduced by thee, he will say, I have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou askest, Hast thou baptism?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have, he says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As long as he says, I have, thou sayest, I will not give.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And do not give, for that which thou wishest to give cannot cleave to me; because what I received cannot be taken away from me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But wait, nevertheless; let me see what thou wouldest teach me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Say, he said, in the first place, I have not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this I have; if I shall say, I have not, I lie; for what I have I have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast not, he says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Teach me that I have it not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: An evil man gave it to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Christ is evil, an evil man did give it to me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, he says, is not evil; but Christ did not give it to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then gave it to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Reply, I know that I received it from Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who gave it to thee, he says, was not Christ, but some traditor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I shall see to it who was the minister; I shall see who was the herald.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Concerning the official, I do not dispute; I give heed to the Judge: and, perchance, in thy objection to the official, thou speakest falsely.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I decline to discuss it; let the Lord of both decide the cause of His own official.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, perhaps, I were to ask for proof, thou couldst give none; indeed, thou liest; it has been proved that thou wert not able to give proof.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I do not place my case on this, lest from my zealous defense of innocent men thou infer that I have placed my hope even on innocent men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the men be what they may, I received from Christ, I was baptized by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No, he says; not Christ, but that bishop baptized thee, and that bishop communicates to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Christ I have been baptized, I know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How dost thou know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove taught me, which John saw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O evil kite, thou mayest not tear me from the bowels of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I am numbered among the members of the dove, because what the dove taught, this I know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest to me, This man or that baptized thee: by means of the dove it is said to me and to thee, “This is He which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Which shall I believe, the kite or the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Tell me certainly, that thou mayest be confounded by that lamp by which also were the former enemies confounded, who were like to thee, the Pharisees, who, when they questioned the Lord by what authority He did those things: “I also,” said He, “will ask you this question, Tell me, the baptism of John, whence is it? from heaven, or of men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they, who were preparing to spread their wiles, were entangled by the question, and began to debate with themselves, and say, “If we shall answer, It is from heaven, He will say unto us, Wherefore did ye not believe him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John had said of the Lord, “Behold the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sin of the world!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then do you inquire by what authority I act?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O wolves, what I do, I do by the authority of the Lamb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that you may know the Lamb, why do you not believe John, who said, “Behold the Lamb of God, who taketh away the sin of the world”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They, then, knowing what John had said regarding the Lord, said among themselves, “If we shall say that John’s baptism is from heaven, He will say unto us, Wherefore then did ye not believe him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we shall say, It is of men, the people will stone us; for they hold John as a prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, they feared men; hence, they were confounded to confess the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Darkness replied with darkness; but they were overcome by the light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what did they reply?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We know not;” regarding that which they knew, they said, “We know not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord said, “Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the first enemies were confounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who was the lamp?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can we prove that he was the lamp?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We can prove it; for the Lord says: “He was a burning and a shining lamp.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Can we prove also that the enemies were confounded by him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the psalm: “I have prepared,” he says, “a lamp for my Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His enemies I will clothe with shame.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As yet, in the darkness of this life, we walk by the lamp of faith: let us hold also to the lamp John, and let us confound by him the enemies of Christ; indeed, let Christ Himself confound His own enemies by His own lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us put the question which the Lord put to the Jews, let us ask and say, “The baptism of John, whence is it? from heaven, or of men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What will they say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark, if they are not as enemies confounded by the lamp.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What will they say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If they shall say, Of men, even their own will stone them; but if they shall say, From heaven, let us say to them, Wherefore, then, did ye not believe him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They perhaps say, We believe him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, then, do you say that you baptize, when John says, “This is He which baptizeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it behoveth, they say, the ministers of so great a Judge who baptize, to be righteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And I also say, and all say, that it behoveth the ministers of so great a Judge to be righteous; let the ministers, by all means, be righteous if they will; but if they will not be righteous who sit in the seat of Moses, my Master made me safe, of whom His Spirit said, “This is He which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How did He make me safe?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The scribes and the Pharisees,” He says, “sit in Moses’ seat: what they say, do; but what they do, that do not ye: for they say, and do not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the minister is righteous, I reckon him with Paul, I reckon him with Peter; with those I reckon righteous ministers: because, in truth, righteous ministers seek not their own glory; for they are ministers, they do not wish to be thought judges, they abhor that one should place his hope on them; therefore, I reckon the righteous minister with Paul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what does Paul say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have planted, Apollos watered; but God gave the increase.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither is he that planteth anything, nor he that watereth; but God who giveth the increase.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he who is a proud minister is reckoned with the devil; but the gift of Christ is not contaminated, which flows through him pure, which passes through him liquid, and comes to the fertile earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose that he is stony, that he cannot from water rear fruit; even through the stony channel the water passes, the water passes to the garden beds; in the stony channel it causes nothing to grow, but nevertheless it brings much fruit to the gardens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the spiritual virtue of the sacrament is like the light: both by those who are to be enlightened is it received pure, and if it passes through the impure it is not stained.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the ministers be by all means righteous, and seek not their own glory, but His glory whose ministers they are; let them not say, The baptism is mine; for it is not theirs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them give heed unto John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, John was full of the Holy Spirit; and he had his baptism from heaven, not from men; but how long had he it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said himself, “Prepare ye the way for the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when the Lord was known, Himself became the way; there was no longer need for the baptism of John to prepare the way for the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, however, are they accustomed to say against us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, after John, baptism was given.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For before that question was properly treated in the Catholic Church, many erred in it, both great and good men; but because they were members of the dove, they did not cut themselves off, and in their case that happened which the apostle said, “If in any thing ye are otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence those who separated themselves became unteachable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then are they wont to say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, after John baptism was given; after heretical baptism is it not to be given? because certain who had the baptism of John were commanded by Paul to be baptized, for they had not the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then, say they, dost thou exaggerate the merit of John, and, as it were, underrate the misery of heretics?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I also grant to you that the heretics are wicked; but the heretics gave the baptism of Christ, which baptism John did not give.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I go back to John, and say, “This is he which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John is better than a heretic, just as John is better than a drunkard, as John is better than a murderer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we ought to baptize after the worse because the apostles baptized after the better, whosoever among them were baptized by a drunkard,—I do not say by a murderer, I do not say by the satellite of some wicked man, I do not say by the robber of other men’s goods, I do not say by the oppressor of orphans, or a separater of married persons; I speak of none of these; I speak of what happens every year, of what happens every day; I speak of what all are called to, even in this city, when it is said to them, Let us play the part of the irrational, let us have pleasure, and on such a day as this of the calends of January we ought not to fast: these are the things I speak of, these trifling everyday proceedings;—when one is baptized by a drunkard, who is better?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John or the drunkard?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Reply, if thou canst, that the drunkard is better than John!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This thou wilt never venture to do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do you then, as a sober man, baptize after thy drunkard.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the apostles baptized after John, how much more ought the sober to baptize after the drunkard?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or dost thou say, the drunkard is in unity with me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was not John then, the friend of the Bridegroom, in unity with the Bridegroom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I say to thee thyself, whoever thou art, Art thou better than John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt not venture to say: I am better than John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then let thine own baptize after thee if they are better.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if baptism was administered after John, blush that baptism is not administered after thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou wilt say, But I have and teach the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Acknowledge, then, now the Judge, and do not be a proud herald.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou givest the baptism of Christ, therefore baptism is not administered after thee: after John it was administered, because he gave not the baptism of Christ, but his own; for he had in such manner received it that it was his own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou art then not better than John: but the baptism given through thee is better than that of John; for the one is Christ’s, but the other is that of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that which was given by Paul, and that which was given by Peter, is Christ’s; and if baptism was given by Judas it was Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Judas gave baptism and after Judas baptism was not repeated; John gave baptism, and baptism was repeated after John: because if baptism was given by Judas, it was the baptism of Christ; but that which was given by John, was John’s baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We prefer not Judas to John; but the baptism of Christ, even when given by the hand of Judas, we prefer to the baptism of John, rightly given even by the hand of John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was said of the Lord before He suffered, that He baptized more than John; then it was added: “Howbeit, Jesus Himself baptized not, but His disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, and not He: He by power, they by ministry; they performed the service of baptizing, the power of baptizing remained in Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His disciples, then, baptized, and Judas was still among his disciples: and were those, then, whom Judas baptized not again baptized; and those whom John baptized were they again baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Plainly there was a repetition, but not a repetition of the same baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those whom John baptized, John baptized; those whom Judas baptized, Christ baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner, then, they whom a drunkard baptized, those whom a murderer baptized, those whom an adulterer baptized, if it was the baptism of Christ, were baptized by Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not fear the adulterer, the drunkard, or the murderer, because I give heed unto the dove, through whom it is said to me, “This is He which baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, my brethren, it is madness to say that—I will not say Judas—but that any man was better than he of whom it was said, that “Among those that are born of women, there hath not arisen a greater than John the Baptist.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: No servant then is preferred to him; but the baptism of the Lord, even when given through an evil servant, is preferred to the baptism even of a servant who was a friend.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the sort of persons whom the Apostle Paul mentions, false brethren, preaching the word of God through envy, and what he says of them: “And I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They proclaimed Christ, through envy indeed, but still they proclaimed Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider not the why, but the whom: through envy is Christ preached to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold Christ, avoid envy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not imitate the evil preacher, but imitate the Good One who is preached to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ then was preached by some out of envy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is envy?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A shocking evil.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this evil was the devil cast down; this malignant pest it was which cast him down; and certain preachers of Christ were possessed by it, whom, nevertheless, the apostle permitted to preach.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because they preached Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he who envies, hates; and he who hates, what is said concerning him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen to the Apostle John: “He who hateth his brother is a murderer.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, after John baptism was given, after a murderer baptism was not given; because John gave his own baptism, the murderer gave the baptism of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That sacrament is so sacred that not even the ministration of a murderer pollutes it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not reject John, but rather I believe John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what do I believe John?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that which he learned through the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did he learn through the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This is He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now therefore, brethren, hold this fast and impress it upon your hearts; for if I would more fully explain to-day, Wherefore through the dove? time fails.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I have, I think, to some extent made plain to you, holy brethren, that a matter which had to be learned was instilled into John by means of the dove, a matter with regard to Christ which John did not know, although he already knew Christ; but why it behoved this matter to be pointed out by means of the dove, I would say, were it possible to say it briefly: but because it would take long to say, and I am unwilling to burden you, since I have been helped by your prayers to perform my promise; with the renewed help of your pious attention and good wishes, it will likewise become clear to you, wherefore John with regard to that matter which he learned regarding the Lord, namely, that it is “He which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost,” and that to none of His servants had he transferred the power of baptizing—why this it became him not to learn except through the dove.
